title: Roles and powers of the president
description: Practice your skills in this quiz on the roles and powers of the president.
topic_path: What powers does the president have?
Roles and powers of the president

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an accurate comparison of the expressed powers of the president and the powers of Congress?**

 | Presidential powers | Congressional powers
- | - | -
**A** | Appointing ambassadors | Interpreting the Constitution
**B** | Negotiating treaties | Ratifying treaties
**C** | Issuing signing statements | Levying taxes
**D** | Pardoning impeached officials | Impeaching officials

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
President Bellingham’s administration wants to limit the development of chemical weapons in key nations in the Middle East, but Congress is unlikely to back a formal treaty. 

**Which of the following is an action the president may take to accomplish their policy agenda?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Negotiate an executive agreement with the heads of the Middle Eastern governments
Pass a bill appropriating tax money for research into the harmful effects of chemical weaponry
Persuade the Supreme Court to declare the use of chemical weapons unconstitutional
Use the State of the Union address to persuade Congress to negotiate a treaty with the Middle Eastern governments

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an accurate comparison of the expressed powers of the president and the powers of Congress?**

 | Presidential powers | Congressional powers
- | - | -
**A** | Issuing executive orders | Reviewing lower-court decisions
**B** | Confirming presidential appointments | Creating federal laws
**C** | Nominating ambassadors | Declaring war
**D** | Ratifying treaties| Negotiating treaties

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an accurate comparison of the expressed powers of the president and the powers of Congress?**

 | Presidential powers | Congressional powers
- | - | -
**A** | Nominating ambassadors | Issuing signing statements
**B** | Presenting the State of the Union | Impeaching officials
**C** | Issuing executive orders | Levying taxes
**D** | Enforcing laws | Interpreting the Constitution

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
**In which of the following scenarios is the president most likely to use an executive order to make important policy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Congress cannot reach a compromise
The president’s party controls Congress
The Supreme Court is not in session
The vice president does not support the policy

assessment_items.question_content:
>“The president may be convinced that a piece of legislation is unwise. But his judgment as to its un-wisdom is not a legal reason for his declining to execute it. (It may be a reason to veto it, and one of the objections to signing statements is that they are vetoes not subject to override.) And the president may have a definite view as to what the legislation means, and that view might include conclusions as to its constitutionality, but his is not the view that counts. He may be the commander in chief, but he is not the interpreter in chief.”

>-Stanley Fish, "Who's Afraid of Presidential Signing Statements?" Source: [New York Times](https://opinionator.blogs.nytimes.com/2007/02/04/whos-afraid-of-presidential-signing-statements/)

**Which of the following statements best summarizes Fish’s argument?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Signing statements go beyond the president’s expressed constitutional powers

Signing statements provide legal 
reasons for the president to decline to execute laws
Vetoes should be replaced with signing statements, since they are not subject to override
The president should not execute laws he or she deems unconstitutional

assessment_items.question_content:
**A signing statement is best defined as which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A released letter explaining how the president’s administration plans to interpret the law
A president’s power to reject a law passed by Congress
An international agreement between the president and another country that does not require the consent of the Senate
A presidential order to the executive branch that carries the force of law

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios would be considered a constitutional use of executive power?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The president confers diplomatic recognition on the new nation of South Sudan
The president ratifies a treaty limiting strategic arms development in the United States and Russia
The president declares war on Iraq and Afghanistan
The president impeaches the US ambassador to South Korea for high crimes and misdemeanors

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an accurate comparison of the president’s formal and informal powers?** 

| Formal powers | Informal powers
- | - | -
**A** | Setting the agenda for Congress | Granting pardons
**B** | Declaring war | Delivering the State of the Union address
**C** | Vetoing legislation | Issuing executive orders
**D** | Acting as Commander in Chief | Appointing Supreme Court justices

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an accurate comparison of the president’s formal and informal powers?** 

| Formal powers | Informal powers
- | - | -
**A** | Nominating federal judges | Issuing executive orders
**B** | Negotiating executive agreements | Granting pardons
**C** | Making treaties | Vetoing legislation
**D** | Creating a budget | Issuing signing statements
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
**In which of the following scenarios is the president most likely to use the veto to limit the power of Congress?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: When the opposing party holds a majority in Congress
When the president's party holds a small majority in Congress
When the opposing party holds a supermajority in Congress
When the president's party holds a supermajority in Congress

assessment_items.question_content:
On the last day of its session, Congress passes a law that the president strongly opposes. 
	
**Which of the following may the president do to limit the power of Congress?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Neither sign nor veto the bill, allowing it to die
Declare the law unconstitutional, thereby killing it
Persuade members of Congress to vote against the law
Refuse to allocate tax money to fund the law

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements describes a consideration presidents face when deciding whether or not to issue a veto?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: If the opposing party has a supermajority in Congress, they can vote to override the veto
If the opposing party has a simple majority in Congress, they may choose to refuse to accept the veto
If the president’s party has a supermajority in Congress, they may choose to rule the president’s veto unconstitutional
If the president’s party has a simple majority in Congress, they may choose to issue a signing statement that interprets the law differently

assessment_items.question_content:
**How does the president use the executive branch to overcome legislative gridlock and advance policy goals?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: By directing Cabinet agencies through regulations
By appointing judges who can rule in favor of presidential priorities
By asking the Speaker of the House to bypass committee procedures
By using Senate rules to fast-track bills to floor votes

assessment_items.question_content:
**What best explains how the president advances their policy agenda within the federal government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The president uses members of the executive branch to lead initiatives and implement programs.
The president introduces a bill in Congress and relies on the courts to interpret its meaning.
The president works with the Cabinet and judicial officials to carry out policy goals.
The president relies on the vice president to propose legislation and coordinate with Congress.

assessment_items.question_content:
>The United States faces persistent and increasingly sophisticated malicious cyber campaigns that threaten the public sector, the private sector, and ultimately the American people’s security and privacy. The Federal Government must improve its efforts to identify, deter, protect against, detect, and respond to these actions and actors.

> -[Executive Order 14028](https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2021/05/17/2021-10460/improving-the-nations-cybersecurity), signed by President Joe Biden on May 12, 2021.

**How did President Biden use the executive branch to implement his policy agenda on cybersecurity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: By directing executive agencies to improve cybersecurity protections
By proposing new cybersecurity legislation to Congress for approval and implementation
By collaborating with the judicial branch to interpret existing cybersecurity laws
By delegating cybersecurity responsibilities entirely to state governments without federal oversight
